events.summary: 👉 Follow Trump100 on your podcast app 👈

events.title: Five people stuck in flooded cave in Laos for week found alive
events.summary: Five people who were stuck in a flooded cave in central Laos for more than a week have been found alive, but two others are still missing, rescuers say.

events.title: North Korea tests AI-guided missiles for the first time
events.summary: North Korea has tested missiles guided by artificial intelligence for the first time, according to state media.

events.title: Spanish police search prime minister's party HQ over corruption allegations
events.summary: Police have searched the Madrid headquarters of Spain's ruling Socialist Party over a potential illegal payments scheme.

events.summary: To meet Gisele Pelicot is to meet an icon.

events.summary: Three people have died after an overpass partially collapsed in South Korea, officials said.

events.title: Pope Leo warns about AI and calls for regulation as he quotes from The Lord Of The Rings
events.summary: Pope Leo has called for robust regulation of artificial intelligence (AI) in a sweeping manifesto on safeguarding humankind.

events.title: 3 killed after passenger van hits an elephant in a Ugandan national park
events.summary: Police in Uganda say three people have been killed when the van they were traveling in hit an elephant in a national park

events.title: US has deported thousands of Cubans and Venezuelans to danger in Mexico, Human Rights Watch says
events.summary: The Trump administration has deported nearly 13,000 Cubans, Venezuelans and others to Mexico, where they face cartel violence and limited legal options

events.title: Muslim pilgrims perform Hajj rituals in strong heat as Eid al-Adha celebrations start
events.summary: Pilgrims in Saudi Arabia threw pebbles at a large pillar in a symbolic ritual during the final days of Hajj

events.title: UAE accused of training Colombian mercenaries for Sudan's war
events.summary: Human Rights Watch reports that the United Arab Emirates trained Colombian mercenaries to fight alongside a paramilitary group in Sudan's war

events.title: Exceptionally early heat wave shatters records and brings deaths in Europe
events.summary: A spring heat wave is scorching parts of Western Europe, breaking temperature records and triggering government warnings

events.summary: An Australian government inquiry has heard that a state police force has worked to form a heavily armed rapid response team since the Bondi Beach shooting

events.title: Iran expands restrictions on distribution of news content by international media outlets
events.summary: Iran is expanding restrictions on distribution of news content from the country

events.title: Brazil's Flávio Bolsonaro leans into Trump connection as scandal shakes his presidential bid
events.summary: Brazilian Sen. Flávio Bolsonaro is in Washington, leaning on his family's connection with former U.S. President Donald Trump

events.title: 3 dead in South Korea after collapse at overpass demolition site
events.summary: Three people have been killed and three others injured when an old overpass undergoing demolition partially collapsed in the South Korean capital

events.title: Inquiry into antisemitism in Australia condemns online hatred and bigotry targeting witnesses
events.summary: The head of an inquiry into antisemitism in Australia has condemned online hatred and bigotry targeting Jewish witnesses

events.title: Russia maintains attacks on Ukraine as Kyiv warned to brace for major barrage
events.summary: Ukraine’s air force says Russia launched over 100 drones and two ballistic missiles at Ukraine overnight

events.title: ICC trial for ex-Philippine President Duterte to start in November
events.summary: Judges at the International Criminal Court have set Nov. 30 as the start date for the trial of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte for crimes against humanity

events.title: Dozens of Assad-era chemical weapons found in Syria in recent weeks, watchdog says
events.summary: The Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons says it has found dozens of undeclared chemical bombs and rockets in Syria

events.title: A rare public trial opens in Paris child abuse case as parents seek a national wake-up call
events.summary: Parents’ groups in France are calling for more attention to child abuse scandals as a rare public trial opens in Paris

events.title: Quad foreign ministers hold talks in New Delhi on Indo-Pacific cooperation
events.summary: Foreign ministers from the Quad countries of Australia, India, Japan, and the United States are meeting in New Delhi to strengthen cooperation in the Indo-Pacific and their concern over China’s growing influence in the region

events.title: Berlin police arrest man suspected of being an accomplice to Holocaust Memorial stabbing
events.summary: An alleged accomplice of a man who was convicted of stabbing and seriously wounding a Spanish tourist at Berlin’s Holocaust Memorial last year has been arrested in the German capital

events.title: Planes carrying 19 Australians linked to the Islamic State group land in Melbourne and Sydney
events.summary: Two planes carrying 19 Australian women and children linked to the Islamic State group in Syria have landed in Melbourne and Sydney

events.title: The cruise ship at center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak has to undergo extra cleaning
events.summary: A cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak is undergoing further cleaning before it returns to its home port

events.title: North Korea says it tested new warheads, technology and navigation in latest launches
events.summary: North Korea says its latest launches involved multiple weapons systems, including a nuclear-capable cruise missile that leader Kim Jong Un plans to deploy with front-line units facing South Korea

events.title: Puerto Rico governor announces emergency to fight worsening coastal erosion
events.summary: Puerto Rico’s governor has announced a state of emergency following critical coastal erosion along the U.S. territory’s north coast

events.title: Muslim pilgrims converge on Arafat for prayers and worship as Hajj reaches its peak
events.summary: Muslim pilgrims have gathered on Mount Arafat in Saudi Arabia for the second day of Hajj, considered the pinnacle of the annual pilgrimage

events.title: Hong Kong proposes issuing 10,000 permits for drivers on ride-hailing apps like Uber
events.summary: The Hong Kong government has proposed issuing 10,000 new vehicle permits for online ride-hailing services like Uber

events.title: Starbucks faces backlash in South Korea over ‘Tank Day’ campaign
events.summary: Starbucks is facing backlash in South Korea over a “Tank Day” marketing campaign that evoked the country’s deadly 1980 military crackdown on pro-democracy protesters. In South Korea, Tank Day commemorates the day the country’s then-military government sent troops and tanks to supress mass rallies for democracy.

events.title: From solar to charcoal, Cubans look for alternatives amid outages and fuel shortages
events.summary: Some Cubans who’ve been able to buy solar panels say it’s a game changer, but most rely on other alternatives including charcoal, despite health concerns and the steep cost.

events.title: Absence of USAID likely slowed Ebola detection and response, former officials say
events.summary: The Trump administration hollowed out USAID last year, but the State Department denied the changes have hampered its response.

events.title: Four years after World Cup drama, Gio Reyna gets another shot with Team USA
events.summary: Last week, Gio Reyna and his wife, Chloe, were sitting in a parking lot, outside a smoothie shop in Connecticut, waiting to hear if he’d made Team USA’s World Cup roster.

events.title: Russia pressures U.S., Ukraine’s allies to flee Kyiv, threatening ‘systematic strikes’
events.summary: The warning came just days after the Ukrainian capital faced one of the heaviest bombardments since the start of the Russian invasion.

events.title: Gandalf, Picasso and MLK: Cultural references ground Pope Leo’s AI warning
events.summary: It took a little over a year for Pope Leo XIV to write more than 42,000 words of his first encyclical, which deals with safeguarding human dignity in the age of artificial intelligence.

events.title: ‘I’m from Miami,’ but ‘it’s hot’: Rubio talks Hormuz, trade and searing temperatures in India
events.summary: Summers in New Delhi can be brutal, even for Floridians used to the heat

events.title: Starbucks struggles to quell outrage over ‘Tank Day’ campaign that evoked massacre in South Korea
events.summary: The head of Starbucks in South Korea apologized again as the company faces sustained public backlash against a marketing campaign widely perceived as mocking those who died for the country’s pro-democracy movement.

events.title: Spanish government confirms new case of hantavirus
events.summary: The Spanish government said it has confirmed a new case of hantavirus connected to the cruise ship MV Hondius, which became the center of an outbreak that killed three people earlier this month.

events.title: Ebola patients flee in attacks on DR Congo health facilities, hobbling response
events.summary: Doctors operating on the front lines of the fight against Ebola in Congo are having to deal with attacks on their facilities and fleeing patients.

events.title: DR Congo’s neighbors impose Covid-style measures in push to limit Ebola’s spread
events.summary: Regional concern over the Ebola outbreak intensified as Uganda confirmed three new cases, while neighboring countries stepped up monitoring measures amid fears of cross-border spread.

events.title: Iran says no deal ‘imminent’ despite progress in talks with U.S.
events.summary: Iran warned that an agreement to end the war launched by the U.S. and Israel wasn’t imminent after President Donald Trump raised and then lowered expectations that a deal may be close.

events.title: China’s Huawei touts chip design breakthrough in bid to defy U.S. sanctions
events.summary: Chinese tech giant Huawei said it had achieved a breakthrough that would allow it to make cutting-edge chips within five years, touting the news as a milestone in Beijing’s effort to circumvent U.S. technology restrictions.

events.title: Pope Leo warns AI is fueling conflict, urges world to slow advances
events.summary: Pope Leo XIV has called for AI advances to be slowed and blamed the technology for fueling conflict around the world.

events.title: Allegations of rape on ‘Married at First Sight UK’ rattle the reality-TV industry
events.summary: Allegations of rape and sexual abuse on the British version of the reality show “Married at First Sight” have rattled the industry and led to threats of tighter regulation

events.title: China controls a metal that’s key for the Iran war, sending the U.S. on a global hunt for more
events.summary: The U.S. is burning through weapons stockpiles in Iran, and replacing them will require large amounts of tungsten, a powerful metal whose supply is dominated by China.
